text: Melanie Murk. Healthy nutrition is crucial for the health of individuals and society as a whole. Consumers want to eat healthier, but face obstacles: healthy food is more expensive and less easy and quick to obtain than unhealthy food. This is evident from the consumer research conducted by Panelwizard on behalf of ABN AMRO. Because responsibility for healthy food is fragmented, even more cooperation between supermarkets, producers and government is needed.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Panelwizard's research shows that almost 89 percent of the more than 1,000 respondents consider healthy food to be (very) important. The Wheel of Five is included as a starting point for the definition of healthy food in this study. Despite this intention, consumers encounter several barriers. Price is an important factor; almost 38 percent of respondents indicate that healthy products are too expensive. It is striking that households with young children (44.9 percent) and the age group 18 to 30 years (53.6 percent) in particular find healthy food too expensive.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The Wheel of Five is included as a starting point for the definition of healthy food in this study. Despite this intention, consumers encounter several barriers. Price is an important factor; almost 38 percent of respondents indicate that healthy products are too expensive. It is striking that households with young children (44.9 percent) and the age group 18 to 30 years (53.6 percent) in particular find healthy food too expensive. Convenience and speed also play a role, with 21.3 percent of respondents indicating that healthy food is more difficult to obtain and takes less time to prepare than unhealthy food.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Despite this intention, consumers encounter several barriers. Price is an important factor; almost 38 percent of respondents indicate that healthy products are too expensive. It is striking that households with young children (44.9 percent) and the age group 18 to 30 years (53.6 percent) in particular find healthy food too expensive. Convenience and speed also play a role, with 21.3 percent of respondents indicating that healthy food is more difficult to obtain and takes less time to prepare than unhealthy food. Here too, households with young children (31.5 percent) and the group between 18 and 30 years old (37.3 percent) score relatively high.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Price is an important factor; almost 38 percent of respondents indicate that healthy products are too expensive. It is striking that households with young children (44.9 percent) and the age group 18 to 30 years (53.6 percent) in particular find healthy food too expensive. Convenience and speed also play a role, with 21.3 percent of respondents indicating that healthy food is more difficult to obtain and takes less time to prepare than unhealthy food. Here too, households with young children (31.5 percent) and the group between 18 and 30 years old (37.3 percent) score relatively high. Other important reasons are that consumers find healthy food less tasty (16.7 percent) or find it difficult to find out whether products are healthy due to poor information provision (15.5 percent).

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: It is striking that households with young children (44.9 percent) and the age group 18 to 30 years (53.6 percent) in particular find healthy food too expensive. Convenience and speed also play a role, with 21.3 percent of respondents indicating that healthy food is more difficult to obtain and takes less time to prepare than unhealthy food. Here too, households with young children (31.5 percent) and the group between 18 and 30 years old (37.3 percent) score relatively high. Other important reasons are that consumers find healthy food less tasty (16.7 percent) or find it difficult to find out whether products are healthy due to poor information provision (15.5 percent). While healthy food is associated with complex and long preparation times, unhealthy choices – products that fall outside the Wheel of Five – are available everywhere: in supermarkets, at stations and at fast food chains.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Convenience and speed also play a role, with 21.3 percent of respondents indicating that healthy food is more difficult to obtain and takes less time to prepare than unhealthy food. Here too, households with young children (31.5 percent) and the group between 18 and 30 years old (37.3 percent) score relatively high. Other important reasons are that consumers find healthy food less tasty (16.7 percent) or find it difficult to find out whether products are healthy due to poor information provision (15.5 percent). While healthy food is associated with complex and long preparation times, unhealthy choices – products that fall outside the Wheel of Five – are available everywhere: in supermarkets, at stations and at fast food chains. Unhealthy food is seen as relatively cheap, easy to snack on, often attractively presented and available in many places.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Here too, households with young children (31.5 percent) and the group between 18 and 30 years old (37.3 percent) score relatively high. Other important reasons are that consumers find healthy food less tasty (16.7 percent) or find it difficult to find out whether products are healthy due to poor information provision (15.5 percent). While healthy food is associated with complex and long preparation times, unhealthy choices – products that fall outside the Wheel of Five – are available everywhere: in supermarkets, at stations and at fast food chains. Unhealthy food is seen as relatively cheap, easy to snack on, often attractively presented and available in many places. Shared responsibility needed.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Other important reasons are that consumers find healthy food less tasty (16.7 percent) or find it difficult to find out whether products are healthy due to poor information provision (15.5 percent). While healthy food is associated with complex and long preparation times, unhealthy choices – products that fall outside the Wheel of Five – are available everywhere: in supermarkets, at stations and at fast food chains. Unhealthy food is seen as relatively cheap, easy to snack on, often attractively presented and available in many places. Shared responsibility needed. The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) estimates that more than 8 percent of the burden of disease in the Netherlands can be attributed to unhealthy nutrition.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: While healthy food is associated with complex and long preparation times, unhealthy choices – products that fall outside the Wheel of Five – are available everywhere: in supermarkets, at stations and at fast food chains. Unhealthy food is seen as relatively cheap, easy to snack on, often attractively presented and available in many places. Shared responsibility needed. The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) estimates that more than 8 percent of the burden of disease in the Netherlands can be attributed to unhealthy nutrition. This was evident in a previously published report by ABN AMRO on the role of companies in a healthy society.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Unhealthy food is seen as relatively cheap, easy to snack on, often attractively presented and available in many places. Shared responsibility needed. The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) estimates that more than 8 percent of the burden of disease in the Netherlands can be attributed to unhealthy nutrition. This was evident in a previously published report by ABN AMRO on the role of companies in a healthy society. With the increasing prevalence of obesity and related conditions such as type 2 diabetes, promoting healthy eating habits is [an important challenge](https://www.cpb.nl/economic-perspective-op-gezondheid-en-Prevention).

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Shared responsibility needed. The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) estimates that more than 8 percent of the burden of disease in the Netherlands can be attributed to unhealthy nutrition. This was evident in a previously published report by ABN AMRO on the role of companies in a healthy society. With the increasing prevalence of obesity and related conditions such as type 2 diabetes, promoting healthy eating habits is [an important challenge](https://www.cpb.nl/economic-perspective-op-gezondheid-en-Prevention). The vast majority of consumers believe that they are responsible for their own health, but are strongly influenced by price, marketing and product offering.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM) estimates that more than 8 percent of the burden of disease in the Netherlands can be attributed to unhealthy nutrition. This was evident in a previously published report by ABN AMRO on the role of companies in a healthy society. With the increasing prevalence of obesity and related conditions such as type 2 diabetes, promoting healthy eating habits is [an important challenge](https://www.cpb.nl/economic-perspective-op-gezondheid-en-Prevention). The vast majority of consumers believe that they are responsible for their own health, but are strongly influenced by price, marketing and product offering. Producers and supermarkets influence eating behavior through range, price and advertising.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: This was evident in a previously published report by ABN AMRO on the role of companies in a healthy society. With the increasing prevalence of obesity and related conditions such as type 2 diabetes, promoting healthy eating habits is [an important challenge](https://www.cpb.nl/economic-perspective-op-gezondheid-en-Prevention). The vast majority of consumers believe that they are responsible for their own health, but are strongly influenced by price, marketing and product offering. Producers and supermarkets influence eating behavior through range, price and advertising. The government focuses on information, but is reluctant to implement more compelling price measures, such as a sugar or fat tax, partly because the effectiveness of these measures is strongly disputed.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: With the increasing prevalence of obesity and related conditions such as type 2 diabetes, promoting healthy eating habits is [an important challenge](https://www.cpb.nl/economic-perspective-op-gezondheid-en-Prevention). The vast majority of consumers believe that they are responsible for their own health, but are strongly influenced by price, marketing and product offering. Producers and supermarkets influence eating behavior through range, price and advertising. The government focuses on information, but is reluctant to implement more compelling price measures, such as a sugar or fat tax, partly because the effectiveness of these measures is strongly disputed. Collaboration is even more necessary to make healthy food easier, cheaper and more attractive for everyone. [Read also: Companies take the lead for a healthy society](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/bedrijven-aan-zet-voor-een-gezonde-samenleving.html).

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The vast majority of consumers believe that they are responsible for their own health, but are strongly influenced by price, marketing and product offering. Producers and supermarkets influence eating behavior through range, price and advertising. The government focuses on information, but is reluctant to implement more compelling price measures, such as a sugar or fat tax, partly because the effectiveness of these measures is strongly disputed. Collaboration is even more necessary to make healthy food easier, cheaper and more attractive for everyone. [Read also: Companies take the lead for a healthy society](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/bedrijven-aan-zet-voor-een-gezonde-samenleving.html). Emeritus professor of Nutrition and Health Jaap Seidell referred to a 'wicked problem' during his farewell speech: keeping everyone healthy for as long as possible is a complex and difficult to solve problem that involves contradictions.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Producers and supermarkets influence eating behavior through range, price and advertising. The government focuses on information, but is reluctant to implement more compelling price measures, such as a sugar or fat tax, partly because the effectiveness of these measures is strongly disputed. Collaboration is even more necessary to make healthy food easier, cheaper and more attractive for everyone. [Read also: Companies take the lead for a healthy society](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/bedrijven-aan-zet-voor-een-gezonde-samenleving.html). Emeritus professor of Nutrition and Health Jaap Seidell referred to a 'wicked problem' during his farewell speech: keeping everyone healthy for as long as possible is a complex and difficult to solve problem that involves contradictions. Consumers often find themselves in an environment that encourages them to eat a lot of unhealthy food and exercise little.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The government focuses on information, but is reluctant to implement more compelling price measures, such as a sugar or fat tax, partly because the effectiveness of these measures is strongly disputed. Collaboration is even more necessary to make healthy food easier, cheaper and more attractive for everyone. [Read also: Companies take the lead for a healthy society](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/bedrijven-aan-zet-voor-een-gezonde-samenleving.html). Emeritus professor of Nutrition and Health Jaap Seidell referred to a 'wicked problem' during his farewell speech: keeping everyone healthy for as long as possible is a complex and difficult to solve problem that involves contradictions. Consumers often find themselves in an environment that encourages them to eat a lot of unhealthy food and exercise little. Every party with influence on that environment has a turn, is his plea.

[Read also: Companies take the lead for a healthy society](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/bedrijven-aan-zet-voor-een-gezonde-samenleving.html). Emeritus professor of Nutrition and Health Jaap Seidell referred to a 'wicked problem' during his farewell speech: keeping everyone healthy for as long as possible is a complex and difficult to solve problem that involves contradictions. Consumers often find themselves in an environment that encourages them to eat a lot of unhealthy food and exercise little. Every party with influence on that environment has a turn, is his plea. This is also evident from the consumer research by Panelwizard.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Emeritus professor of Nutrition and Health Jaap Seidell referred to a 'wicked problem' during his farewell speech: keeping everyone healthy for as long as possible is a complex and difficult to solve problem that involves contradictions. Consumers often find themselves in an environment that encourages them to eat a lot of unhealthy food and exercise little. Every party with influence on that environment has a turn, is his plea. This is also evident from the consumer research by Panelwizard. Consumers believe first and foremost that they should provide healthy food themselves and that in the case of children that task lies with parents and caregivers.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Consumers often find themselves in an environment that encourages them to eat a lot of unhealthy food and exercise little. Every party with influence on that environment has a turn, is his plea. This is also evident from the consumer research by Panelwizard. Consumers believe first and foremost that they should provide healthy food themselves and that in the case of children that task lies with parents and caregivers. But like Jaap Seidell, consumers agree that other actors also have an influence.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Every party with influence on that environment has a turn, is his plea. This is also evident from the consumer research by Panelwizard. Consumers believe first and foremost that they should provide healthy food themselves and that in the case of children that task lies with parents and caregivers. But like Jaap Seidell, consumers agree that other actors also have an influence. For example, food producers are identified as responsible by 37 percent of respondents, followed closely behind by the government (35.3 percent) and supermarkets (34.7 percent).

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: This is also evident from the consumer research by Panelwizard. Consumers believe first and foremost that they should provide healthy food themselves and that in the case of children that task lies with parents and caregivers. But like Jaap Seidell, consumers agree that other actors also have an influence. For example, food producers are identified as responsible by 37 percent of respondents, followed closely behind by the government (35.3 percent) and supermarkets (34.7 percent). The catering industry (5.5 percent) is less often assigned a role.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Consumers believe first and foremost that they should provide healthy food themselves and that in the case of children that task lies with parents and caregivers. But like Jaap Seidell, consumers agree that other actors also have an influence. For example, food producers are identified as responsible by 37 percent of respondents, followed closely behind by the government (35.3 percent) and supermarkets (34.7 percent). The catering industry (5.5 percent) is less often assigned a role. Supermarkets bear responsibility.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: But like Jaap Seidell, consumers agree that other actors also have an influence. For example, food producers are identified as responsible by 37 percent of respondents, followed closely behind by the government (35.3 percent) and supermarkets (34.7 percent). The catering industry (5.5 percent) is less often assigned a role. Supermarkets bear responsibility. More than 80 percent of consumers visit the supermarket to do their shopping for dinner, our research shows.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: For example, food producers are identified as responsible by 37 percent of respondents, followed closely behind by the government (35.3 percent) and supermarkets (34.7 percent). The catering industry (5.5 percent) is less often assigned a role. Supermarkets bear responsibility. More than 80 percent of consumers visit the supermarket to do their shopping for dinner, our research shows. Supermarkets therefore have a great social responsibility.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The catering industry (5.5 percent) is less often assigned a role. Supermarkets bear responsibility. More than 80 percent of consumers visit the supermarket to do their shopping for dinner, our research shows. Supermarkets therefore have a great social responsibility. They largely determine what consumers are offered, at what price, and how visible those products are.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Supermarkets bear responsibility. More than 80 percent of consumers visit the supermarket to do their shopping for dinner, our research shows. Supermarkets therefore have a great social responsibility. They largely determine what consumers are offered, at what price, and how visible those products are. The design of supermarkets is often such that consumers are guided in their choices, for example through checkout promotions, volume deals or striking placement of products on the shelf.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: More than 80 percent of consumers visit the supermarket to do their shopping for dinner, our research shows. Supermarkets therefore have a great social responsibility. They largely determine what consumers are offered, at what price, and how visible those products are. The design of supermarkets is often such that consumers are guided in their choices, for example through checkout promotions, volume deals or striking placement of products on the shelf. Consumers usually first come into contact with the fruit and vegetable department, which encourages a healthy choice, but critics argue that approximately 80 percent of the supermarket offering falls outside the Wheel of Five.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Supermarkets therefore have a great social responsibility. They largely determine what consumers are offered, at what price, and how visible those products are. The design of supermarkets is often such that consumers are guided in their choices, for example through checkout promotions, volume deals or striking placement of products on the shelf. Consumers usually first come into contact with the fruit and vegetable department, which encourages a healthy choice, but critics argue that approximately 80 percent of the supermarket offering falls outside the Wheel of Five. This makes it more difficult for many consumers to make structurally healthier choices, even when the intention is there.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: They largely determine what consumers are offered, at what price, and how visible those products are. The design of supermarkets is often such that consumers are guided in their choices, for example through checkout promotions, volume deals or striking placement of products on the shelf. Consumers usually first come into contact with the fruit and vegetable department, which encourages a healthy choice, but critics argue that approximately 80 percent of the supermarket offering falls outside the Wheel of Five. This makes it more difficult for many consumers to make structurally healthier choices, even when the intention is there. Specialty food stores, such as butchers, greengrocers or bakers, only reach a limited part of the population due to generally higher prices and a less accessible location.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The design of supermarkets is often such that consumers are guided in their choices, for example through checkout promotions, volume deals or striking placement of products on the shelf. Consumers usually first come into contact with the fruit and vegetable department, which encourages a healthy choice, but critics argue that approximately 80 percent of the supermarket offering falls outside the Wheel of Five. This makes it more difficult for many consumers to make structurally healthier choices, even when the intention is there. Specialty food stores, such as butchers, greengrocers or bakers, only reach a limited part of the population due to generally higher prices and a less accessible location. Consumers do most of their shopping in the supermarket (one-stop shopping).

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Consumers usually first come into contact with the fruit and vegetable department, which encourages a healthy choice, but critics argue that approximately 80 percent of the supermarket offering falls outside the Wheel of Five. This makes it more difficult for many consumers to make structurally healthier choices, even when the intention is there. Specialty food stores, such as butchers, greengrocers or bakers, only reach a limited part of the population due to generally higher prices and a less accessible location. Consumers do most of their shopping in the supermarket (one-stop shopping). This underlines that supermarkets, which serve a broad audience, hold a key position in making healthy food accessible to everyone.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: This makes it more difficult for many consumers to make structurally healthier choices, even when the intention is there. Specialty food stores, such as butchers, greengrocers or bakers, only reach a limited part of the population due to generally higher prices and a less accessible location. Consumers do most of their shopping in the supermarket (one-stop shopping). This underlines that supermarkets, which serve a broad audience, hold a key position in making healthy food accessible to everyone. Food companies also have their say.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Specialty food stores, such as butchers, greengrocers or bakers, only reach a limited part of the population due to generally higher prices and a less accessible location. Consumers do most of their shopping in the supermarket (one-stop shopping). This underlines that supermarkets, which serve a broad audience, hold a key position in making healthy food accessible to everyone. Food companies also have their say. Food companies also bear a major responsibility in promoting a healthy living environment.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Consumers do most of their shopping in the supermarket (one-stop shopping). This underlines that supermarkets, which serve a broad audience, hold a key position in making healthy food accessible to everyone. Food companies also have their say. Food companies also bear a major responsibility in promoting a healthy living environment. The pressure on the sector is increasing as the development of obesity is not reversed.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: This underlines that supermarkets, which serve a broad audience, hold a key position in making healthy food accessible to everyone. Food companies also have their say. Food companies also bear a major responsibility in promoting a healthy living environment. The pressure on the sector is increasing as the development of obesity is not reversed. Respondents in the Panelwizard study indicate that they pay particular attention to nutritional value (sugars, fats, salt, proteins), energy and ingredients to determine which food is healthy.

The pressure on the sector is increasing as the development of obesity is not reversed. Respondents in the Panelwizard study indicate that they pay particular attention to nutritional value (sugars, fats, salt, proteins), energy and ingredients to determine which food is healthy. One in three respondents say they use the Wheel of Five for this purpose.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Food companies also bear a major responsibility in promoting a healthy living environment. The pressure on the sector is increasing as the development of obesity is not reversed. Respondents in the Panelwizard study indicate that they pay particular attention to nutritional value (sugars, fats, salt, proteins), energy and ingredients to determine which food is healthy. One in three respondents say they use the Wheel of Five for this purpose. The much-discussed Nutri-score, the food logo that uses colors to indicate how healthy a product is within a product group, is less used.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The pressure on the sector is increasing as the development of obesity is not reversed. Respondents in the Panelwizard study indicate that they pay particular attention to nutritional value (sugars, fats, salt, proteins), energy and ingredients to determine which food is healthy. One in three respondents say they use the Wheel of Five for this purpose. The much-discussed Nutri-score, the food logo that uses colors to indicate how healthy a product is within a product group, is less used. One in six respondents looks at the Nutri score.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Respondents in the Panelwizard study indicate that they pay particular attention to nutritional value (sugars, fats, salt, proteins), energy and ingredients to determine which food is healthy. One in three respondents say they use the Wheel of Five for this purpose. The much-discussed Nutri-score, the food logo that uses colors to indicate how healthy a product is within a product group, is less used. One in six respondents looks at the Nutri score. Because many products fall outside the Wheel of Five and consumers buy them because they are mainly tasty, the Nutri score is ideally suited to let consumers choose the 'better alternative' within a product group and to stimulate the food industry to improve the composition of these products.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: One in three respondents say they use the Wheel of Five for this purpose. The much-discussed Nutri-score, the food logo that uses colors to indicate how healthy a product is within a product group, is less used. One in six respondents looks at the Nutri score. Because many products fall outside the Wheel of Five and consumers buy them because they are mainly tasty, the Nutri score is ideally suited to let consumers choose the 'better alternative' within a product group and to stimulate the food industry to improve the composition of these products. This improvement was previously implemented with the Product Composition Improvement Agreement.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The much-discussed Nutri-score, the food logo that uses colors to indicate how healthy a product is within a product group, is less used. One in six respondents looks at the Nutri score. Because many products fall outside the Wheel of Five and consumers buy them because they are mainly tasty, the Nutri score is ideally suited to let consumers choose the 'better alternative' within a product group and to stimulate the food industry to improve the composition of these products. This improvement was previously implemented with the Product Composition Improvement Agreement. Further improvement of products – for example by continuing to reduce sugars, fats and salt – can help consumers eat healthier.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: One in six respondents looks at the Nutri score. Because many products fall outside the Wheel of Five and consumers buy them because they are mainly tasty, the Nutri score is ideally suited to let consumers choose the 'better alternative' within a product group and to stimulate the food industry to improve the composition of these products. This improvement was previously implemented with the Product Composition Improvement Agreement. Further improvement of products – for example by continuing to reduce sugars, fats and salt – can help consumers eat healthier. The affordability of healthy food is also crucial; By offering affordable, convenient, healthier options, the food industry can also help consumers.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Or by introducing farm-to-table concepts, where fresh, seasonal products are brought directly from the farm to the plate. The Nutrition Center provides [practical tips](https://www.voedingscentrum.nl/kindermenu) how catering entrepreneurs can make the children's menu healthier in the direction of the Wheel of Five. The role of government. In addition, the government intervenes with various policy instruments such as communication, organization, legal management and financial incentives. For example, JOGG teams (started as Youth at a Healthy Weight) have been working locally since 2010 on a healthier living environment by sharing knowledge with municipalities, which can then adjust their policy and put this into practice with JOGG.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The Nutrition Center provides [practical tips](https://www.voedingscentrum.nl/kindermenu) how catering entrepreneurs can make the children's menu healthier in the direction of the Wheel of Five. The role of government. In addition, the government intervenes with various policy instruments such as communication, organization, legal management and financial incentives. For example, JOGG teams (started as Youth at a Healthy Weight) have been working locally since 2010 on a healthier living environment by sharing knowledge with municipalities, which can then adjust their policy and put this into practice with JOGG. In the National Prevention Agreement, which was concluded in 2018, binding agreements have been made with, among others, social organizations and the business community to prevent obesity.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The role of government. In addition, the government intervenes with various policy instruments such as communication, organization, legal management and financial incentives. For example, JOGG teams (started as Youth at a Healthy Weight) have been working locally since 2010 on a healthier living environment by sharing knowledge with municipalities, which can then adjust their policy and put this into practice with JOGG. In the National Prevention Agreement, which was concluded in 2018, binding agreements have been made with, among others, social organizations and the business community to prevent obesity. Various actions have been taken based on the agreement, such as the reduction of sugar in soft drinks and the limitation of marketing aimed at children.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: In addition, the government intervenes with various policy instruments such as communication, organization, legal management and financial incentives. For example, JOGG teams (started as Youth at a Healthy Weight) have been working locally since 2010 on a healthier living environment by sharing knowledge with municipalities, which can then adjust their policy and put this into practice with JOGG. In the National Prevention Agreement, which was concluded in 2018, binding agreements have been made with, among others, social organizations and the business community to prevent obesity. Various actions have been taken based on the agreement, such as the reduction of sugar in soft drinks and the limitation of marketing aimed at children. In 2024, the consumption tax will increase on non-alcoholic drinks, such as fruit and vegetable juices, lemonade and non-alcoholic beer, to encourage healthier choices.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: For example, JOGG teams (started as Youth at a Healthy Weight) have been working locally since 2010 on a healthier living environment by sharing knowledge with municipalities, which can then adjust their policy and put this into practice with JOGG. In the National Prevention Agreement, which was concluded in 2018, binding agreements have been made with, among others, social organizations and the business community to prevent obesity. Various actions have been taken based on the agreement, such as the reduction of sugar in soft drinks and the limitation of marketing aimed at children. In 2024, the consumption tax will increase on non-alcoholic drinks, such as fruit and vegetable juices, lemonade and non-alcoholic beer, to encourage healthier choices. The consumption tax on mineral water was abolished.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: In the National Prevention Agreement, which was concluded in 2018, binding agreements have been made with, among others, social organizations and the business community to prevent obesity. Various actions have been taken based on the agreement, such as the reduction of sugar in soft drinks and the limitation of marketing aimed at children. In 2024, the consumption tax will increase on non-alcoholic drinks, such as fruit and vegetable juices, lemonade and non-alcoholic beer, to encourage healthier choices. The consumption tax on mineral water was abolished. However, the above measures have not yet led to a desired reduction in obesity.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Various actions have been taken based on the agreement, such as the reduction of sugar in soft drinks and the limitation of marketing aimed at children. In 2024, the consumption tax will increase on non-alcoholic drinks, such as fruit and vegetable juices, lemonade and non-alcoholic beer, to encourage healthier choices. The consumption tax on mineral water was abolished. However, the above measures have not yet led to a desired reduction in obesity. Help consumers with a healthier food offering.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: In 2024, the consumption tax will increase on non-alcoholic drinks, such as fruit and vegetable juices, lemonade and non-alcoholic beer, to encourage healthier choices. The consumption tax on mineral water was abolished. However, the above measures have not yet led to a desired reduction in obesity. Help consumers with a healthier food offering. The call to the various parties – supermarkets, food companies, catering establishments – is clear: help consumers by contributing to a healthier food supply and include health in the business model even more often, in collaboration with each other and with the government.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The consumption tax on mineral water was abolished. However, the above measures have not yet led to a desired reduction in obesity. Help consumers with a healthier food offering. The call to the various parties – supermarkets, food companies, catering establishments – is clear: help consumers by contributing to a healthier food supply and include health in the business model even more often, in collaboration with each other and with the government. Options include reformulating product compositions and expanding the product range, encouraging healthy choices and making food quality transparent.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: However, the above measures have not yet led to a desired reduction in obesity. Help consumers with a healthier food offering. The call to the various parties – supermarkets, food companies, catering establishments – is clear: help consumers by contributing to a healthier food supply and include health in the business model even more often, in collaboration with each other and with the government. Options include reformulating product compositions and expanding the product range, encouraging healthy choices and making food quality transparent. For example, it requires considerable effort from food manufacturers to reduce the amount of sugar and fat in products, but at the same time keep them 'tasty' so that consumers do not walk away.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: Help consumers with a healthier food offering. The call to the various parties – supermarkets, food companies, catering establishments – is clear: help consumers by contributing to a healthier food supply and include health in the business model even more often, in collaboration with each other and with the government. Options include reformulating product compositions and expanding the product range, encouraging healthy choices and making food quality transparent. For example, it requires considerable effort from food manufacturers to reduce the amount of sugar and fat in products, but at the same time keep them 'tasty' so that consumers do not walk away. Supermarkets face the same challenge, in collaboration with their suppliers and as sellers of private labels.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: The call to the various parties – supermarkets, food companies, catering establishments – is clear: help consumers by contributing to a healthier food supply and include health in the business model even more often, in collaboration with each other and with the government. Options include reformulating product compositions and expanding the product range, encouraging healthy choices and making food quality transparent. For example, it requires considerable effort from food manufacturers to reduce the amount of sugar and fat in products, but at the same time keep them 'tasty' so that consumers do not walk away. Supermarkets face the same challenge, in collaboration with their suppliers and as sellers of private labels. For example, Albert Heijn is working with its suppliers to reduce sugar in private label products.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: More than 22 percent of all respondents have become more aware of nutrition and health through social media (via influencers); for the 18-30 age group this is much higher at 37 percent. And more than 17 percent have changed their diet or nutritional style, such as choosing vegan food or using less sugar. Here too, the proportion of young people is much higher at 30 percent. Conclusion. Healthy food is a complex problem in which consumers are helped by a joint approach by all stakeholders.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text: And more than 17 percent have changed their diet or nutritional style, such as choosing vegan food or using less sugar. Here too, the proportion of young people is much higher at 30 percent. By focusing on affordability, convenience, variety and information, food companies, catering and supermarkets can make a significant contribution to promoting a healthier lifestyle for everyone.

metadata.source_title: Consumers want to eat healthy, but are confronted with price and convenience

text:
Today's store is more than just a pantry. It offers customers advice and inspiration and is the place to be in contact with customers. Due to the Ukraine crisis and significant inflation, consumer confidence has fallen sharply and this affects consumer behavior. Retailers are also faced with significant cost increases in the areas of purchasing, rent, personnel costs, Covid recovery payments and energy.
